name: X: Search Posts
description: Search X for posts from one handle, optionally constrained to a date window, and return candidate post cards. Use when you want posts from a single author either recent (latest/top) or within a specific interval. Do not use to read one post (`x_open_post`) or to expand a thread (`x_thread`).

browser_automation.description: Navigates a fresh tab directly to the X search URL (consent-overlay-safe), scrolls to accumulate cards, then extracts a structured digest.

sequences.name: x_search_posts
sequences.description: Build a search URL -> scroll -> extract candidate post metadata.

optional_variables.name: since_date
optional_variables.description: Inclusive lower-bound in YYYY-MM-DD, empty to skip

optional_variables.name: until_date
optional_variables.description: Exclusive upper-bound in YYYY-MM-DD, empty to skip

optional_variables.name: timeline_mode
optional_variables.description: One of top | latest | live (default latest)

optional_variables.name: max_posts
optional_variables.description: Upper bound on posts to extract (default 40)

steps.name: Open x.com in a fresh tab so direct search navigation is cookie-safe
Build the search URL and navigate to it
steps.script: const cleanArg = (v) => { const t = String(v == null ? '' : v).trim(); return /^\{[a-zA-Z0-9_]+\}$/.test(t) ? '' : t; }; const handle = cleanArg('{handle}').replace(/^@+/, ''); const since = cleanArg('{since_date}'); const until = cleanArg('{until_date}'); const rawMode = cleanArg('{timeline_mode}').toLowerCase(); const mode = ['top','latest','live'].includes(rawMode) ? rawMode : 'latest'; const modeFlag = mode === 'top' ? 'top' : (mode === 'live' ? 'live' : 'live'); const parts = ['from:' + handle]; if (since) parts.push('since:' + since); if (until) parts.push('until:' + until); const q = parts.join(' '); const url = 'https://x.com/search?q=' + encodeURIComponent(q) + '&src=typed_query&f=' + modeFlag; setTimeout(() => window.location.assign(url), 0); return { handle, since, until, mode, search_url: url };

steps.name: Wait for post cards
steps.selector: main article[data-testid='tweet'], main [data-testid='cellInnerDiv'] article, main article

steps.name: Scroll until enough candidate posts are visible
steps.item_selector: main article[data-testid='tweet'], main [data-testid='cellInnerDiv'] article, main article

steps.name: Extract candidate posts
steps.item_selector: main article[data-testid='tweet'], main [data-testid='cellInnerDiv'] article, main article

help.summary: Search X for posts from one handle, optionally within a date window, across top/latest/live tabs.

parameters.name: since_date
parameters.description: Inclusive lower-bound date. Omit for no lower bound.

parameters.name: until_date
parameters.description: Exclusive upper-bound date. Omit for no upper bound.

parameters.name: timeline_mode
parameters.description: Which X search tab to land on (defaults to latest, which is the most common use case).

parameters.name: max_posts
parameters.description: Upper bound on extracted posts (engine-side limit, default ~40).

examples.description: Most-recent posts from @elonmusk with no date window.

examples.command: rzn-browser run x search-posts --param handle="felixrieseberg" --param since_date="2026-03-10" --param until_date="2026-03-18" --param timeline_mode="live"
examples.description: Posts from @felixrieseberg between 2026-03-10 and 2026-03-18 on the Live tab.

help.returns: Array of post cards (author, text, posted_at, post_url, reply_count, repost_count, like_count, view_count) plus a search_url echo.
notes: Navigation is done via setTimeout + location.assign from the home page, which avoids the consent-overlay gotcha of typing into the landing search box.
Empty optional params are detected via the standard cleanArg helper.
timeline_mode=top uses the Top tab; latest and live both land on the Live tab.
